frameworks.name: grokking
frameworks.one_line: delayed generalization where representations keep improving after behavior plateaus

frameworks.name: margin maximization
frameworks.one_line: class representations continue to separate during overtraining

frameworks.name: neural population decoding
frameworks.one_line: linear readout from sensory cortex tracks hidden representational change

core_question: Does task-relevant representation learning continue in sensory cortex after behavior saturates?
key_assumptions: behavioral plateau need not imply representational plateau
linear decodability indexes task-relevant population structure
held-out generalization tests reveal latent learning
manipulations: amount of training extended past behavioral ceiling (overtraining)
key_findings: decoding accuracy from posterior piriform populations increases during overtraining
generalization performance on held-out tests improves after behavioral saturation
previously misclassified examples become correctly classified later in training
class representations continue to separate in cortex over overtraining
interpretations: hidden learning during overtraining takes the form of approximate margin maximization
sensory cortex exhibits grokking-like delayed representational progress
